title: Box Office: Avatar: Fire and Ash Remains No. 1 in Fifth Weekend as 28 Years Later: Bone Temple Stumbles
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash dominated the MLK weekend box office, earning an estimated $17.8 million (4-day total), maintaining its number one position for the fifth consecutive weekend. The film's strong performance was fueled by its IMAX and premium format appeal. Meanwhile, 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple significantly underperformed pre-release expectations. Initial tracking had suggested the horror sequel would earn $20-22 million over the 4-day holiday period. Instead, it garnered only $15 million total ($13 million for the three-day period), finishing in second place. The gap between predictions and actual performance represents a notable shortfall for Sony, especially given the previous film's $30 million opening. Despite the box office disappointment, the film received positive critical reception with a 92% critics' score on Rotten Tomatoes and an A- CinemaScore from audiences. The sequel's stronger critical than commercial performance suggests potential word-of-mouth recovery could help its theatrical run.
